title: I need help with Recreating N64 songs from Neon Genesis Evangelion 1999
display_title: I need help with Recreating N64 songs from Neon Genesis Evangelion 1999
wikitext:
[]: I need help with Recreating N64 songs from Neon Genesis Evangelion (1999)             Quote Post by Penguin_029032 » Thu Apr 14, 2022 9:13 pm I'm using LMMS since it is an Open Source Music Software. After downloading N64 Tools, Seq64, and a ROM I own, and what I've seen from the data or experienced while decompiling feels hard to recreate or try to use custom music as high-quality rips. Can you guys help me make a guide for a list of songs on what Instrument banks I need to map? So I wouldn't get lost while editing. It's sorta complicated cuz according to the music sequences, it had too many instruments which find it misleading during my research, or maybe I'm wrong.

[]: Re: I need help with Recreating N64 songs from Neon Genesis Evangelion (1999)             Quote Post by bloopymicrowave » Thu Sep 29, 2022 9:23 pm See; While I am stupid, I can try to help some, despite having no experience.. If it lets you use sound fonts, find the Mario 64 sound font since Eva 64 and Mario 64 share a sound font (hence Komm, Susser Tod being pretty funny and sounding like a Mario 64 song) Hope this helps you.

Article: I need help with Recreating N64 songs from Neon Genesis Evangelion 1999
[]: I need help with Recreating N64 songs from Neon Genesis Evangelion (1999)             Quote Post by Penguin_029032 » Thu Apr 14, 2022 9:13 pm I'm using LMMS since it is an Open Source Music Software. After downloading N64 Tools, Seq64, and a ROM I own, and what I've seen from the data or experienced while decompiling feels hard to recreate or try to use custom music as high-quality rips. Can you guys help me make a guide for a list of songs on what Instrument banks I need to map? So I wouldn't get lost while editing. It's sorta complicated cuz according to the music sequences, it had too many instruments which find it misleading during my research, or maybe I'm wrong.

[]: Re: I need help with Recreating N64 songs from Neon Genesis Evangelion (1999)             Quote Post by bloopymicrowave » Thu Sep 29, 2022 9:23 pm See; While I am stupid, I can try to help some, despite having no experience.. If it lets you use sound fonts, find the Mario 64 sound font since Eva 64 and Mario 64 share a sound font (hence Komm, Susser Tod being pretty funny and sounding like a Mario 64 song) Hope this helps you.
